t: SAP & AI: Copilot to Autonomous Enterprise
d: How SAP is transforming with AI and agentic AI — the verified 3-year arc from Joule the copilot (2024) to reasoning agents (2025) to the Autonomous Enterprise (2026). Explained for beginners.

t: Lead to Cash — The Customer Process in SAP
d: Follow the customer process step by step — from a marketing lead to a closed sale to cash in the bank — mapped to SAP CX and S/4HANA. Explained for beginners.

t: Record to Report — The Finance Process in SAP
d: Follow the finance process step by step — from a transaction to published financial statements — and see exactly which SAP product handles each stage.

t: Hire to Retire — The HR Process in SAP
d: Follow the HR process step by step — from posting a job to onboarding, paying, developing and offboarding — mapped to SAP SuccessFactors modules.

t: What SAP Automates — The Process Map
d: Paying suppliers, closing the books, hiring people, shipping goods — see which SAP product handles each step of every core business process. Explained for beginners.
k: S/4HANA Finance SAC SuccessFactors Ariba S/4HANA IBP Digital Mfg SAP CX

t: Build & Integrate — SAP's IT Platform
d: The meta-process: how IT extends, integrates and automates everything else using SAP BTP and SAP Build. From idea to deployed extension, step by step.

t: Source to Pay — The Procurement Process in SAP
d: Follow the procurement process step by step — from finding a supplier to paying the invoice — mapped to SAP Ariba and S/4HANA. Explained for beginners.

t: Plan to Deliver — SAP Supply Chain
d: Follow the supply chain process step by step — forecast, plan, make, store, ship — mapped to SAP IBP, Digital Manufacturing, EWM and TM. Explained for beginners.

t: SAP for a CFO / Finance Director — Explained
d: Which SAP products does a CFO or Finance Director actually use? S/4HANA Finance, Analytics Cloud, Concur, Group Reporting — explained simply, with the BTP angle.

t: SAP for a CHRO / HR Director — Explained
d: Which SAP products does an HR leader use? SuccessFactors (Employee Central, Recruiting, Learning, Payroll) and Fieldglass — the people leader's view of SAP, explained.

t: SAP for a CIO / IT Director — Explained
d: Which SAP products does a CIO own? BTP, Integration Suite, deployment strategy, clean core, security — the technology leader's view of SAP, explained.

t: SAP for a COO / Operations Head — Explained
d: Which SAP products does an operations leader use? IBP (planning), Digital Manufacturing, EWM (warehouse), TM (transportation) — the operations view of SAP, explained.

t: SAP for a CPO / Procurement Head — Explained
d: Which SAP products does a procurement leader use? Ariba (source-to-pay), Fieldglass (external workforce), Concur (T&E), Business Network — explained for beginners.

t: SAP for a Developer / BTP Builder — Explained
d: Which SAP products does a developer use? SAP Build (low-code), BTP pro-code, Joule Studio, Integration Suite — the builder's view of SAP, explained for beginners.

t: Who Uses SAP? The Landscape by Role
d: A CFO touches different SAP products than an HR head or a developer. Find the SAP products that live in your world — explained by role, for beginners.
k: S/4HANA Finance SAC Concur SAP BTP Integration Suite SuccessFactors Fieldglass Ariba

t: SAP for Enterprise — RISE & Full Suite
d: What SAP does a large enterprise (2,000+ staff) use? RISE with SAP plus the full suite — S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba, IBP, CX, all on BTP. Explained for beginners.

t: SAP by Company Size — Every Scale
d: A 30-person startup and a 200,000-person multinational both run SAP — but completely different products. See which SAP fits which size, explained for beginners.
k: SAP Business One GROW with SAP RISE with SAP Full suite

t: SAP for Mid-Market — GROW with SAP Explained
d: What SAP does a mid-sized, growing company use? GROW with SAP — standardized cloud ERP on S/4HANA Public Edition, live in weeks. Explained for beginners.

t: SAP for Small Business — Business One
d: What SAP does a small business (under ~50 staff) use? SAP Business One — one affordable system for accounting, sales, purchasing and inventory. Explained simply.

t: Hybrid SAP — The Real-World Mix, Explained
d: Most real companies don't run one SAP model — they run a hybrid mix of on-premise, cloud, and line-of-business apps, stitched together with BTP. Explained for beginners.

t: Where SAP Runs — 4 Deployment Models
d: On-premise, private cloud (RISE), public cloud (GROW), or hybrid — the single biggest decision in any SAP project, explained simply for beginners.
k: S/4HANA on-prem Legacy ECC RISE S/4HANA Private Ed. GROW S/4HANA Public Ed. Two-tier ERP BTP bridge

t: On-Premise SAP — Explained Simply
d: On-premise SAP means running it on your own servers in your own data center. Who chooses it, why, and how it compares — for total beginners.

t: Private Cloud — RISE with SAP, Explained
d: RISE with SAP is SAP's private cloud: your own dedicated S/4HANA tenant, run by SAP. The standard path for big companies leaving on-premise. Explained for beginners.

t: Public Cloud — GROW with SAP, Explained
d: GROW with SAP is SAP's public cloud: standardized S/4HANA Public Edition, go live in weeks. For companies new to SAP Cloud ERP — midsize and large. Explained for beginners.

t: SAP Jargon Buster — Every Term, Plain English
d: Every SAP acronym and buzzword, explained in one sentence of plain English. ERP, S/4HANA, BTP, RISE, GROW, Clean Core, Fiori, Joule and more.

t: SAP Landscape — Explained for Everyone
d: A beginner-friendly map of the entire SAP product universe — explained by deployment model, role, business process, and company size. No jargon left undefined.
k: On-Premise RISE GROW Hybrid CFO CIO CHRO Developer

t: The Full SAP Product Map — Every Pillar
d: The entire SAP product universe on one page: the SAP Business Suite architecture, all 14 product pillars, and how every product connects — explained for beginners.
k: Finance Procurement Manufacturing Integration Suite SAP Build HANA Cloud Employee Central Recruiting

t: What is SAP Ariba? Procurement Network
d: SAP Ariba is SAP's source-to-pay procurement suite and supplier network — find suppliers, run sourcing, manage contracts, buy and pay. Explained for beginners.

t: What is SAP BTP? The Tech Platform
d: SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) is where all custom work lives — integration, low-code app building (SAP Build), databases and AI. Explained for beginners.

t: What is SAP Concur? Travel & Expense
d: SAP Concur is SAP's travel and expense management product — book trips, capture receipts, auto-check policy, reimburse and post to finance. Explained for beginners.

t: What is SAP CX? Customer Experience, Explained
d: SAP CX (Customer Experience) is SAP's customer-facing suite — commerce, marketing, sales and service in one connected view. Explained for beginners.

t: What is SAP IBP? Supply Chain Planning
d: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning) is SAP's cloud supply chain planning hub — demand forecasting, S&OP, inventory optimization. Explained for beginners.

t: What is SAP Joule? Business AI & Agents
d: SAP Joule is SAP's AI — from the embedded copilot to 200+ autonomous agents that run finance, HR and supply chain tasks. Joule, Joule Studio, the Autonomous Suite, explained for beginners.

t: What is SAP S/4HANA? The Core ERP, Explained
d: SAP S/4HANA is SAP's flagship ERP — the operating system of a business. What it does, who uses it, where it runs (RISE/GROW), and how it connects. For beginners.

t: What is SAP Signavio? Process Suite
d: SAP Signavio maps and X-rays how a business actually works — process modeling plus process mining to find bottlenecks. Explained for beginners.

t: What is SAP SuccessFactors? HR Suite
d: SAP SuccessFactors is SAP's cloud HR suite — the whole employee journey from hire to retire. Modules, who uses it, how it connects. Explained for beginners.
